titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/73 (2)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=692 status=live modified=2017-02-24 13:35:06.099155+00 | translation moa=693 status=live modified=2017-02-24 13:35:08.738133+00

p.cl:
Vniuersis Christi fidelibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Walterus filius Alani domini Regis dapifer Salutem in
domino .

me ad honorem dei . et pro animabus dominorum meorum regum Scotie et pro salute
mea et . B . uxoris mee et omnium antecessorum et heredum meorum dedisse concessisse et hac presenti carta
mea confirmasse deo et Monasterio de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus et imperpetuum serui
turis territorium de Mauchelin et ecclesiam Sancti Michaelis in eo sitam . Concessi quidem eis . et hac mea
carta confirmaui totum prefatum territorium per suas diuisas contentas Scilicet in carta mea quam inde habent
Monachi . vt habeant omnia contenta intra predictas diuisas cum omnibus liberatatibus et pertinentiis
ad idem tenementum de iure uel longa consuetudine spectantibus . Ego quidem et heredes mei waran
tizabimus et defendemus predictis Monachis contra omnes homines . et eos in pace possidere faciemus omnia
supradicta cum suis pertinentiis . Tenenda et habenda libera penitus et quieta ab omni terreno seruitio
et omni consuetidine et exactione et demanda . Insuper quinque Marcas annuas ad quas mihi et heredibus meis
soluendas predicti Monachi pro eadem terra singulis annis secundum tenorem carte mee . et carte patris mei
tenebantur eisdem Monachis in perpetuum caritatiue remisi et eos ab illarum solutione pro me et om
nibus heredibus et successoribus meis liberos feci et inperpetuum inmunes . A uenatione etiam et auium cap
tione quas ego et antecessores mei in libera terra dicte ecclesie de Mauchelin consueuimus excercere et ab omni
usu cuiuslibet artis et negotii secularis eandem terram ecclesie cum suis pertinentiis liberam feci et pe
nitus exhoneraui .

Et ut hec mea donatio et confirmatio sine omni impedimento firmatur et stabiles
fiant presens scriptum Sigilli mei appositione roboraui .

Hiis testibus Domino Iohanne de Mackuswel
Bernardo fraser . Adam filio Gilberti . Reynaldo de crauford . Rogero filio Glaii . Roberto logan .
Roberto de crauford . Nigello de heriz . Roberto clerico et Multis aliis .
To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear this writing, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord king, sends greeting in the Lord.

to the honour of God, and for the souls of my lords the kings of Scotland, and for my own soul, and the soul of my wife and of all my ancestors and heirs, to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and the monastery at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God now and for ever in the future, the territory of Mauchline, and the church of St Michael to be found there. I have indeed granted to them, and have made firm by this my charter, the whole territory, mentioned before, by its own boundaries, that is, as they are contained in my charter which the monks have concerning it, so that they should have everything contained within the boundaries already said, with all liberties and pertinents that belong to the same holding by right or long-standing custom. I and my heirs shall warrant and defend the said monks against all men, and make them possess in peace all the things said above, with their pertinents. To be held and had entirely free and quit from every earthly service and every custom and exaction and demand. In addition, the five merks which the aforesaid monks were bound to have to pay to me and and my heirs in return for the same land each year according to the words of my charter and the charter of my father, to the same monks, in the spirit affection and charity, I have, for my part and for all my heirs and successors, perpetually remitted and made them free and immune for ever from the payment of them. I have also freed and entirely released the same land and its pertinents from hunting and the capture of birds which I and my ancestors were accustomed to excercise in the free land of the said church of Mauchline and from the practice of any art and business whatever.
And so that my donation and confirmation should be made firm and become lasting, without any impediment, I have strengthened the present writing by the application of my seal.

With these witnesses: the lord John of Maxwell, Berhard Fraser, Adam son of Gilbert, Reginald of Crawford, Roger son of Glay, Robert Logan, Robert of Crawford, Nigel de Heriz, Robert the clerk, and many others.
